chunks.text: Stereo. H C J D A 38. JUDGMENT SHEET LAHORE HIGH COURT MULTAN BENCH MULTAN JUDICIAL DEPARTMENT W.P. No.11759 of 2024 Mst. Saima Rani Vs. Addl. District Judge, etc. J U D G M E N T Date of Hearing: 14.10.2025 Petitioner by: Prince Rehan Iftikhar Sheikh, Advocate. Mr. Muhammad Adeel Shah, Advocate. Respondent No.3 by: Mr. Muhammad Zubair-ul- Hassan Chishti, Advocate. Anwaar Hussain, J. Petitioner seeks restoration of the decree passed by the Family Court whereby her claim for dower (Haq Mahr) was decreed to the extent of a five marla house, while her claim regarding dowry articles was partly accepted. Both the Courts below are concurrent on the question of dowry articles, but at variance on the issue of dower. The Family Court through its judgment dated 11.03.2024 decreed the suit holding that the petitioner was entitled to a five marla house as Haq Mahr. However, the Appellate Court below, vide judgment dated 04.06.2024, set aside that finding and dismissed the claim of the petitioner to that extent. 2. Learned counsel for the petitioner contends that the Nikah Nama is a registered document and carries a presumption of correctness. In column No.16 thereof, a five marla house is specifically recorded as Haq Mahr. It is argued that the Appellate Court below ignored this entry and instead proceeded on an assumption that since property belonging to respondent No.3 (“the respondent”) had been W.P. No.11759 of 2024 - 2 - transferred to the petitioner ten days before the marriage, by way of sale, such transfer represented the discharge of Haq Mahr. Learned counsel submits that this reasoning is wholly conjectural and contrary to the record. 3.

No.11759 of 2024 - 2 - transferred to the petitioner ten days before the marriage, by way of sale, such transfer represented the discharge of Haq Mahr. Learned counsel submits that this reasoning is wholly conjectural and contrary to the record. 3. Conversely, learned counsel for the respondent argues that the marriage between the parties was not arranged and the respondent had been emotionally influenced by the petitioner. It is contended that while under that emotional sway, the respondent executed a sale deed in favour of the petitioner, ten days prior to the marriage, and that transaction effectively represented discharge of Haq Mahr obligation. Hence, the decision of the Appellate Court below is in accordance with law. 4. In rebuttal, with regard to the sale deed, the petitioner’s stance is that there was a prior acquaintance between the families, and the respondent intended to sell his property, which the petitioner’s father purchased and got it transferred in the petitioner’s name. As the petitioner had affection for the respondent, their marriage ultimately took place, albeit against the wishes of the family of the respondent and therefore, the respondent started residing in the same house alongwith the petitioner and her parents. 5. Arguments heard. Record perused. 6. This case presents an interesting factual and legal situation. It is indeed unusual that a couple, if not previously related, would enter into a property transaction only ten days before their marriage, and subsequently the same property becomes the subject of dispute as to whether it was a sale transaction or discharge of dower obligation prior to nikah. Yet, the legal question before this Court is not whether such a transaction appears curious, but whether the entry in the W.P. No.11759 of 2024 - 3 - registered Nikah Nama can be disregarded on the basis of conjecture or assumptions drawn from a prior sale deed between the spouses. 7.

Yet, the legal question before this Court is not whether such a transaction appears curious, but whether the entry in the W.P. No.11759 of 2024 - 3 - registered Nikah Nama can be disregarded on the basis of conjecture or assumptions drawn from a prior sale deed between the spouses. 7. The Nikah Nama, being a registered document, enjoys a presumption of truth. In present case, Column No.16 of the Nikah Nama expressly mentions a “five marla house” as Haq Mahr. There is no note or recital anywhere in that document to the effect that such Mahr had already been paid/transferred through any sale deed prior to the solemnization of Nikah. Equally, the respondent in his written statement did not plead that the property transferred ten days prior to the marriage, by way of sale, was intended to be Haq Mahr; rather, his stance was that no such Mahr was ever settled and that he had been deceived into executing a sale deed. Para No.1 of the written statement reads as under: ’’ ہی ہک رقفہ ربمن1 رعدیضوعیٰ رصف تکارخی اکنح یک دح تک درسک ےہ۔ ہیقب رقفہ طلغ ےہ میلست ہن رنیقی یک ش ادی دنسپ یک ےہ فLove Marriage ےہ۔ دماعہیلع یسلیم رہش ےس اکم رضوری متخ رک ےک اےنپ رھگ احلص وپر اج راہ اھت ہک ایک داتھکی ےہ ہک ومرٹ اسلکیئ اک اڈیسکین ٹ وہا ےہ سج ںیم اتک وعرت اور ہچب وسار ےھت دماعہیلع اگڑی روک رک زویمخں یک ادماد رکت ا اچاتہ ےہ ہک اتک اور ومرٹ اسلکیئ آرک راتک ےہ اس رپ اتک اور ڑلیک وسار وہیت ےہ وج وشر رک ریہ وہیت ےہ ات ا یج ومرٹ اسلکیئ روںیک زیمخ وہےن وایل ریمے وکسل یک رچیٹ ےہ دماعہیلع زیمخ ہچب اور وعر ت وک اینپ اگڑی ںیم ڈال رک اتپسہل ےل اجت ا اچاتہ ےہ وت وج ڑلیک اےنپ ت اپ ےک اسھت یھت وہ دمہیع یھب وہ یھب اگڑی دماعہیلع ھٹیب اجیت ےہ اہیں ےس دمہیع اور دماعہیلع یکLove Story رشوع وہیت ےہLove Story رشوع وہےت وہےئ ت ات الماقوتں ودعہ ودیع تک چنہپ اجیت ےہ دماعہیلع اےنپ رھگ واولں ےس وچری اپھچ رک دمہیع ےس ش ادی رک اتیل ےہ اور دمہیع اور رھگ واےل دمہیع یک وخاشہ رپ دمہیع وریغہ ےک رھگ ںیم’’رھگ وجایئ ‘‘ کے وطر رپ راہشئ پ رپک ر وہ اج تکا وکیئ رشط قح المہ ر ےط ہن وہیئ۔ وکیئ اسامن زیہج دمہیع وک ہن دتکا اجتکا ےہ دمہیع یک وکیئ رخ صتکگی ہن وہیئ ےہ۔‘‘ (Emphasis supplied) Having taken above quoted position, the respondent could not subsequently derive advantage from the sale transaction, prior to the marriage, by portraying it as satisfaction of Haq Mahr.

Equally, the respondent in his written statement did not plead that the property transferred ten days prior to the marriage, by way of sale, was intended to be Haq Mahr; rather, his stance was that no such Mahr was ever settled and that he had been deceived into executing a sale deed. Para No.1 of the written statement reads as under: ’’ ہی ہک رقفہ ربمن1 رعدیضوعیٰ رصف تکارخی اکنح یک دح تک درسک ےہ۔ ہیقب رقفہ طلغ ےہ میلست ہن رنیقی یک ش ادی دنسپ یک ےہ فLove Marriage ےہ۔ دماعہیلع یسلیم رہش ےس اکم رضوری متخ رک ےک اےنپ رھگ احلص وپر اج راہ اھت ہک ایک داتھکی ےہ ہک ومرٹ اسلکیئ اک اڈیسکین ٹ وہا ےہ سج ںیم اتک وعرت اور ہچب وسار ےھت دماعہیلع اگڑی روک رک زویمخں یک ادماد رکت ا اچاتہ ےہ ہک اتک اور ومرٹ اسلکیئ آرک راتک ےہ اس رپ اتک اور ڑلیک وسار وہیت ےہ وج وشر رک ریہ وہیت ےہ ات ا یج ومرٹ اسلکیئ روںیک زیمخ وہےن وایل ریمے وکسل یک رچیٹ ےہ دماعہیلع زیمخ ہچب اور وعر ت وک اینپ اگڑی ںیم ڈال رک اتپسہل ےل اجت ا اچاتہ ےہ وت وج ڑلیک اےنپ ت اپ ےک اسھت یھت وہ دمہیع یھب وہ یھب اگڑی دماعہیلع ھٹیب اجیت ےہ اہیں ےس دمہیع اور دماعہیلع یکLove Story رشوع وہیت ےہLove Story رشوع وہےت وہےئ ت ات الماقوتں ودعہ ودیع تک چنہپ اجیت ےہ دماعہیلع اےنپ رھگ واولں ےس وچری اپھچ رک دمہیع ےس ش ادی رک اتیل ےہ اور دمہیع اور رھگ واےل دمہیع یک وخاشہ رپ دمہیع وریغہ ےک رھگ ںیم’’رھگ وجایئ ‘‘ کے وطر رپ راہشئ پ رپک ر وہ اج تکا وکیئ رشط قح المہ ر ےط ہن وہیئ۔ وکیئ اسامن زیہج دمہیع وک ہن دتکا اجتکا ےہ دمہیع یک وکیئ رخ صتکگی ہن وہیئ ےہ۔‘‘ (Emphasis supplied) Having taken above quoted position, the respondent could not subsequently derive advantage from the sale transaction, prior to the marriage, by portraying it as satisfaction of Haq Mahr. This contradictory stance defeats his own defence and the case of the W.P. No.11759 of 2024 - 4 - respondent falls under the clutches of maxim Allegans Contraria Non Est Audiendus (A person who alleges things contradictory to each other is not to be heard) disentitling the respondent to any relief. Moreover, it is settled principle of law that evidence beyond pleadings has no value. 8. Suffice to observe that the law does not prohibit a man from selling property to a woman whom he later marries. However, when a registered Nikah Nama specifically records the subject matter of Haq Mahr, and there is no pleading or proof of its prior discharge, the presumption attached to such document must prevail. The Appellate Court below, while overlooking this settled principle, rendered its findings on the basis of assumption. 9. Insofar as findings to the extent of dowry articles are concerned, both the Courts below have concurrently held in favour of the petitioner and awarded certain dowry articles or alternate value of Rs.200,000/-. No misreading and non-reading of evidence could be pointed out by learned counsel for the petitioner. In case reported as M. Hamad Hassan v. Mst. Isma Bukhari and 2 others (2023 SCMR 1434) the Hon’ble Supreme Court of Pakistan has held that findings of fact are not to be interfered with in constitutional jurisdiction unless shown to be perverse, arbitrary, or based on misreading or non-reading of evidence. No such infirmity has been pointed out in the present case. 10. In view of the above discussion, the finding of the Appellate Court below to the extent of disallowing the claim of Haq Mahr only is unsustainable.

No such infirmity has been pointed out in the present case. 10. In view of the above discussion, the finding of the Appellate Court below to the extent of disallowing the claim of Haq Mahr only is unsustainable. The judgment and decree of the Family Court dated 11.03.2024 is restored. 11. Petition is allowed in above terms. (ANWAAR HUSSAIN) JUDGE Approved for reporting. Judge Order was announced in Open Court and dictated on the same date, however, finalized/signed on 30.10.2025. *Maqsood*
